velocity_vs_rigour_quote: Design and grade four formative assessments; Weekly formative assessments: Each week: academic assessment task in exam conditions, 60–90 minutes
velocity_vs_rigour_reasoning: Multiple rigour signals (assessments, grading, exam conditions, feedback) with no velocity language.
domain_risk_quote: Lecturer in Business Analytics and Statistics
domain_risk_reasoning: Educational teaching role with no financial reporting, compliance, or regulatory consequences.
data_team_maturity_quote: Design and deliver seminars adopting the flipped classroom approach based on London School of Economics material
data_team_maturity_reasoning: The program is built on established curriculum and methodology (not greenfield) with multiple pedagogical components, suggesting an established but moderately-scaled teaching infrastructure.
jd_authorship_quote: Teach 22 two-hour weekly seminars; Design and grade four formative assessments; Provide approximately 15 hours of individual tutoring
jd_authorship_reasoning: Specific numbers (22 seminars, 2 hours, 4 assessments, 15 hours) and detailed pedagogical methods indicate hiring manager knowledge.
stakeholder_orientation_quote: Aimed at managers who need to be 'more inquisitive, more precise, more accurate in their statements, more selective in their use of data, more critical of advice given to them.'
stakeholder_orientation_reasoning: The role serves to develop data literacy and analytical capability in learners who will become data-informed decision makers and consumers of analytics.
autonomy_level_quote: Design and deliver seminars adopting the flipped classroom approach based on London School of Economics material
autonomy_level_reasoning: While the lecturer designs seminars and assessments, the curriculum content, pedagogical framework, course structure, and volume are all pre-specified, indicating execution within defined constraints rather than strategic direction-setting.
greenfield_vs_fix_quote: Design and deliver seminars adopting the flipped classroom approach based on London School of Economics material
ai_role_quote: Lecturer in Business Analytics and Statistics
ai_role_reasoning: Educational teaching role with no reference to AI tools, GenAI infrastructure, or AI-assisted work.
testing_framing_quote: Design and grade four formative assessments
testing_framing_reasoning: Assessment refers to educational grading of student learning, not technical testing, quality ownership, or data contracts.
loss_aversion_framing_quote: Aimed at managers who need to be 'more inquisitive, more precise, more accurate in their statements, more selective in their use of data, more critical of advice given to them.'
loss_aversion_framing_reasoning: Concern about analytical accuracy and avoiding poor data-driven decisions signals trust in analysis, but lacks explicit compliance or operational reliability framing.
